documentation.file_docstring: OpenAI TTS Provider Generates speech audio using OpenAI's text-to-speech API. Handles audio format conversion for telephony (mu-law 8kHz). Best practices from OpenAI docs: - Use gpt-4o-mini-tts for intelligent realtime applications (supports instructions) - Use tts-1 for lower latency, tts-1-hd for higher quality - Use marin or cedar voices for best quality - Use pcm or wav format for fastest response times @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/text-to-speech
inline_comments: OpenAI TTS configuration.
TTS model: - gpt-4o-mini-tts: newest, supports instructions for tone/style control (recommended) - tts-1: lower latency - tts-1-hd: higher quality

Voice to use. For best quality, use marin or cedar. All 13 voices: alloy, ash, ballad, coral, echo, fable, nova, onyx, sage, shimmer, verse, marin, cedar Note: tts-1/tts-1-hd only support: alloy, ash, coral, echo, fable, onyx, nova, sage, shimmer
Instructions for speech style (only works with gpt-4o-mini-tts model). Examples: "Speak in a cheerful tone", "Talk like a sympathetic customer service agent"
Supported OpenAI TTS voices (all 13 built-in voices). For best quality, use marin or cedar. Note: tts-1 and tts-1-hd support a smaller set.
OpenAI TTS Provider for generating speech audio.
Default to gpt-4o-mini-tts for intelligent realtime applications
Default to coral - good balance of quality and natural tone
Generate speech audio from text. Returns raw PCM audio data (24kHz, mono, 16-bit).
Build request body
Generate speech and convert to mu-law format for Twilio. Twilio Media Streams expect 8kHz mono mu-law audio.
Get raw PCM from OpenAI (24kHz, 16-bit signed LE, mono)
Resample from 24kHz to 8kHz
Encode to mu-law
Resample 24kHz PCM to 8kHz using linear interpolation. Input/output: 16-bit signed little-endian mono.
Calculate position in input (3:1 ratio)
Linear interpolation between samples
Last sample
Clamp value to 16-bit signed integer range.
Convert 8-bit mu-law to 16-bit linear PCM. Useful for decoding incoming audio.
mu-law is transmitted inverted
Chunk audio buffer into 20ms frames for streaming. At 8kHz mono, 20ms = 160 samples = 160 bytes (mu-law).

functions.name: synthesize
functions.doc: Generate speech audio from text.     Returns raw PCM audio data (24kHz, mono, 16-bit).

functions.name: synthesizeForTwilio
functions.doc: Generate speech and convert to mu-law format for Twilio.     Twilio Media Streams expect 8kHz mono mu-law audio.

functions.name: resample24kTo8k
functions.doc: Resample 24kHz PCM to 8kHz using linear interpolation.   Input: 16-bit signed little-endian mono.

functions.name: clamp16
functions.doc: Clamp value to 16-bit signed integer range.

functions.name: mulawToLinear
functions.doc: Convert 8-bit mu-law to 16-bit linear PCM.   Useful for decoding incoming audio.

functions.name: chunkAudio
functions.doc: Chunk audio buffer into 20ms frames for streaming.   At 8kHz mono, 20ms = 160 samples = 160 bytes (mu-law).

classes.name: OpenAITTSProvider
classes.doc: OpenAI TTS Provider for generating speech audio.
